(A) Approximately 150 miles (240km) off the coast
(B) Exactly 700km from the coastline
(C) Positioned at the Bahrain naval station in the Gulf
(D) Stationed in the eastern Mediterranean near Souda Bay

explanation: The article states that European Sentinel-2 satellite images show the Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea "around 150 miles (240km) off the coast of Oman."

(A)  
</final_answer>

(B) €100bn
(C) €200bn
(D) €75bn
(E) €125bn
explanation: Mark Rutte pointed out that by 2029, the German defense budget will be "€150bn," which he described as a "staggering amount."
model_output:
In his remarks, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte projected that Germany’s defence budget would reach approximately **€160 billion by 2029**. ([nato.int](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/01/26/remarks-by-nato-secretary-general-mark-rutte-at-the-meeting-of-the-european-parliaments-committee-on-security-and-defence?utm_source=openai))

Thus, the closest option among the ones given is:

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NATO Transcript: “Germany … will spend 70 billion on defence in 2021. You will spend 160 billion … in 2029.” ([nato.int](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/01/26/remarks-by-nato-secretary-general-mark-rutte-at-the-meeting-of-the-european-parliaments-committee-on-security-and-defence?utm_source=openai))  
- Politico: Germany’s pledged budget of “€153 billion … by 2029” ([politico.eu](https://www.politico.eu/article/germany-pledges-to-hit-3-5-percent-defense-spending-target-by-2029/?utm_source=openai))

===============================================

Today is February 16, 2026. Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche previously stated that three million pages of Epstein-related files were not released for what specific reason?
(A) To protect the reputations of high-profile political figures
(B) Because the documents were improperly redacted by technical error
(C) Due to deliberative process privilege regarding prosecution decisions
(D) Because they contained internal memos from the Southern District of New York
(E) Due to personal medical files, graphic depictions of child abuse, or material jeopardizing investigations
explanation: Todd Blanche stated the pages were withheld "due to the existence of personal medical files, graphic depictions of child abuse or other material that would jeopardise investigations."
model_output:
Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche said that the ~3 million pages of Epstein-related files were *not released* because they contained:

- personally identifiable information of victims
- personal medical files
- graphic depictions of child abuse (or child sexual abuse material)
- information that could jeopardize ongoing investigations ([thewrap.com](https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/public-policy-legal/jeffrey-epstein-files-3-million-documents-released-doj-redactions/?utm_source=openai))

That corresponds to option **(E)**.

Here’s how the other options compare:

A) Blanche explicitly denied that the documents were being withheld to protect reputations of political figures. Supreme among redaction motivations was protection of victims rather than shielding high-profile people. ([thewrap.com](https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/public-policy-legal/jeffrey-epstein-files-3-million-documents-released-doj-redactions/?utm_source=openai))

B) There was no claim of a “technical error” in making redactions; redactions were deliberate based on legal/privacy/investigative considerations. ([nbcchicago.com](https://www.nbcchicago.com/news/national-international/epstein-files-justice-department-news-conference-todd-blanche/3883772/?utm_source=openai))

C) While some documents are withheld under legal privileges (like attorney-client privilege, work product or deliberative process privilege), Blanche emphasized that the bulk were withheld because of victim privacy, medical files, child abuse, or active investigations—not solely deliberative process. ([thewrap.com](https://www.thewrap.com/industry-news/public-policy-legal/jeffrey-epstein-files-3-million-documents-released-doj-redactions/?utm_source=openai))

D) There was no indication that the reason was simply that they were internal memos from the SDNY; SDNY isn’t singled out in those claims. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/deputy-attorney-general-todd-blanche-news-conference-30-1-2026/?utm_source=openai))

So the correct option is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 16, 2026. Which two deceased musicians were specifically mentioned as appearing in the released Epstein files?
(A) Janis Joplin and Elvis Presley
(B) John Lennon and Jimi Hendrix
(C) Whitney Houston and Prince
(D) David Bowie and Freddie Mercury
(E) Janis Joplin and Jim Morrison
explanation: The article states: "But the list also includes the names of deceased musicians Janis Joplin and Elvis Presley."
model_output:
Based on the most recent releases of the Epstein files, the deceased musician specifically mentioned is **Michael Jackson** ([abc.net.au](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-14/entertainment-celebrities-in-epstein-files/106317858?utm_source=openai)). None of the names listed in the given options (except Jackson) match those documented in the files.

Let’s review each option:

(A) Janis Joplin and Elvis Presley — Neither appears in the known released Epstein files.  
(B) John Lennon and Jimi Hendrix — No evidence they are mentioned in the files.  
(C) Whitney Houston and Prince — Not documented in the files so far.  
(D) David Bowie and Freddie Mercury — Not mentioned in the released documents.  
(E) Janis Joplin and Jim Morrison — Also not present in the files.

None of the pairs from the options match the correctly identified deceased musician (Michael Jackson) plus another. Since **Michael Jackson** appears, but none of the options include his name paired with another deceased musician from the options, the correct response is that *none of the above options* is accurate.

<final_answer>  
None of the above  
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:  
- ABC News: “Michael Jackson can be seen in various photos in the files … image … Jackson is with Bill Clinton and Diana Ross …” ([abc.net.au](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-14/entertainment-celebrities-in-epstein-files/106317858?utm_source=openai))  
- Wikipedia: “Michael Jackson … was an American singer … photo released … showed the singer posing with Epstein …” ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_people_named_in_the_Epstein_files?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 16, 2026. The Environmental Defense Fund estimates that by 2055, the increase in emissions could lead to how many premature deaths?
(A) 7.5 to 18 billion
(B) 10 million
(C) 15,400 to 58,000
(D) 1.3 trillion
(E) 2,400 to 5,000
explanation: The article states: "The Environmental Defense Fund estimates that, by 2055, the increase in emissions could lead to between 15,400 and 58,000 premature deaths."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

**Reasoning:**

The EDF fact sheet states that U.S. climate and clean air actions taken over recent years are projected to “save almost **190,000 lives** by **2055**.” ([edf.org](https://www.edf.org/media/recent-us-climate-and-clean-energy-actions-will-save-tens-thousands-lives-deliver-trillions?utm_source=openai))

None of the other options closely match that figure. Options (B) 10 million and (D) 1.3 trillion are vastly larger; option (E) 2,400 to 5,000 is far smaller; and option (A) 7.5 to 18 billion is also much too high. The only viable match is (C) 15,400 to 58,000 — though that's still lower than EDF’s estimate. But among the options provided, (C) is the closest.

Today is February 16, 2026. Which US Senator warned at the Munich Security Conference that the freedom and fairness of the 2026 midterm elections are at stake?
(A) Senator Mark Kelly
(B) Senator Ruben Gallego
(C) Senator Thom Tillis
(D) Senator Jeanne Shaheen
(E) Senator Mark Warner
explanation: The article states: "Virginia Senator Mark Warner warned that the president's talk of nationalising elections... mean the freedom and fairness of November's midterm congressional elections are at stake."
model_output:
Based on recent reporting, it was **Senator Mark Warner** (option E) who warned at the Munich Security Conference that the freedom and fairness of the 2026 midterm elections are at stake. ([sg.news.yahoo.com](https://sg.news.yahoo.com/trump-gone-three-years-top-001656035.html?utm_source=openai))  

Here’s a brief outline of how this conclusion was reached:

- A news article from *Yahoo News Singapore* reports that “Virginia Senator **Mark Warner warned that the president’s talk of nationalising elections and instituting stringent identity requirements for voting via executive order mean the freedom and fairness of November’s midterm congressional elections are at stake.” ([sg.news.yahoo.com](https://sg.news.yahoo.com/trump-gone-three-years-top-001656035.html?utm_source=openai))  
- None of the other listed senators (Mark Kelly, Ruben Gallego, Thom Tillis, Jeanne Shaheen) were identified in the sources as making that specific warning at Munich. Ruben Gallego, for example, called for a national strike under certain scenarios, but he was **not** the one quoted using that exact phrasing at the Munich Security Conference. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/33e720b820e61db9d5e478e63b4a4dc7?utm_source=openai))  

So, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Yahoo News Singapore: “Virginia Senator Mark Warner warned that the president’s talk … mean the freedom and fairness of November’s midterm congressional elections are at stake.” ([sg.news.yahoo.com](https://sg.news.yahoo.com/trump-gone-three-years-top-001656035.html?utm_source=openai))  
- The Guardian / related reports identifying Gallego’s actions, but not that phrasing at MSC. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/33e720b820e61db9d5e478e63b4a4dc7?utm_source=openai))
